mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance above baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals, or forward returns are negative in over 50% of co-occurring positive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output that improves corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC drops below zero when industrial production growth exceeds 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy when inflation rises alongside strong wage growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series, or forward returns turn negative when payroll growth exceeds 300k while unemployment falls.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion that lifts corporate revenue projections and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in over 50% of quarters with GDP growth above 2%, or the signal loses significance during periods of supply chain constraints.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when supply weakens alongside inflation, or forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances.
